programme.title: Capítulo 81
programme.desc: Giovanni gives his support to Mia. Fearing the Lodge might hurt Julieta, Miguel breaks up with her. By request of Diego, Facundo turns down Roberta.

programme.title: Capítulo 82
programme.desc: Joaquín breaks up with Pilar because she won't sleep with him. Vico thinks that Mia told Esteban about the love letters she's been sending him. Roberta gets manipulated by Diego.

programme.title: Capítulo 83
programme.desc: Valeria pretends to be a photographer for the school to get close to Mia. Miguel warns Mia about Esteban. Roberta makes her choice of which band to stay in.

programme.title: Capítulo 84
programme.desc: Miguel and Esteban get into a fist fight. Joaquín breaks up with Vico by lying that the Lodge might come after her. However, Pilar tells Vico that Joaquín and her are dating again.

programme.title: Capítulo 85
programme.desc: Miguel admonishes Julieta for going out with her former boyfriend, who is dangerous. Valeria tells Franco she spent the day with Mia. Paola tries to seduce Diego.

programme.title: Capítulo 86
programme.desc: Miguel asks Teo to help get him in as a member at the Lodge. Mia finds out Vanessa is really the woman dating her father, Valeria. Paola is hired by León to sleep with Diego.

programme.title: Capítulo 87
programme.desc: Diego reveals to Paola he's a virgin. Miguel is asked by Franco to find Mia, who has run away after she finds out her father and Valeria had a history.

programme.title: Capítulo 88
programme.desc: Miguel comes up with a plan to get Rodrigo to invite Teo into the Lodge. Celina tells Mia that Valeria could tell her everything she want to know about her mother.

programme.title: Capítulo 89
programme.desc: Valeria tells Mía the truth about her mother. Celina is feeling smothered by Augusto. Pilar and Joaquin get back together. Téo gets a meeting with the Lodge. Diego and Paola get close. Paolo lies to León about her and Diego.

programme.title: Capítulo 90
programme.desc: Julieta breaks up with Miguel once she figures out he is in love with Mia. Teo and Miguel pretend to fight. Franco gets upset with Valeria for telling Mia about her mom.

programme.title: Punk Rock God
programme.desc: Stacy and Clinton meet Patrick, a charming “brewery dad” eager to swap his t-shirts and clogs for the edgy punk rock style he idolized as a kid. Struggling with confidence, Patrick has hesitated to embrace his true self. As Clinton and Stacy guide him through this transformation, he rediscovers his identity and finally steps into the spotlight, fulfilling his teenage dream of ultimate cool.

programme.title: 90s Preppy
programme.desc: Clinton and Stacy welcome Selena, a goth content creator seeking a dramatic style transformation. Having worn an all-black aesthetic since middle school, Selena realizes that it hinders her ability to connect with others and looks for guidance to uncover a more approachable style that reflects her evolving identity.

programme.title: Eclectic Bohemian
programme.desc: Aspiring artist Akemi has left a corporate job to pursue her dream as a visionary artist, but she struggles with personal style and craves her parents' approval. Enter Stacy and Clinton, ready to create a bold, artsy look that truly reflects her creative spirit. Together, they empower Akemi to embrace her vibrant identity, boosting her confidence to thrive as the artist she was meant to be.

programme.title: Breaking Gender Norms
programme.desc: Clinton and Stacy take on their most exciting challenge yet with Alan, a recently transitioned trans man seeking a bold, androgynous style. After years of prioritizing comfort over self-expression, Alan is ready to embrace a look that defies traditional norms. Together, they craft a style that brings Alan a true joy that is uniquely his own, breaking the rules of fashion along the way.

programme.title: Flashback: Mike And Gloria's Wedding - Pt. Ii
programme.desc: The family continues to reminisce on Mike and Gloria's second wedding anniversary and they recall the actual day Mike and Gloria got married. As Archie and Mike's Uncle Casimir continue to butt heads, Edith must gather up strength to tell Gloria about 'the birds and the bees'.

programme.title: Sammy's Visit
programme.desc: To get some extra cash, Archie has gotten a job driving a taxi for Bert Munson. Archie is in for a big surprise when one of his passengers turns out to be Sammy Davis Jr. Sammy inadvertently leaves his briefcase behind and arrangements are made for him to retrieve the briefcase at the Bunker house. As usual, Archie's ignorance and bigotry are full force as he nervously awaits Sammy's arrival.

programme.title: Edith, The Judge
programme.desc: One Sunday afternoon, Archie comes home from the laundromat in fury. It seems that he overloaded one of the machines causing the machine to eat his clothes and cause a flood. The irate proprietor of the laundromat, soon stops by the Bunker home to get Archie to pay for the damages but Archie refuses. Soon Edith is called in to act as judge to decide who is at fault in the matter.

programme.title: Archie Is Jealous
programme.desc: Gloria is reading over an old love letter that Mike had sent her. This leads to a conversation between Gloria and Edith concerning an old flame of Edith's with whom she once spent a weekend. However, when Archie hears of Edith's supposed innocent weekend, he hits the roof and demands that Edith not keep any secrets from him.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Edith's cousin, Maude, invites Archie and Edith to the wedding of her daughter, Carol. However, Carol and Maude are not looking forward to Archie, since the man she is marrying happens to be Jewish. Later, the men and the women have their respective bachelor and bachelorette parties, but a grumpy Archie decides to stay in the motel. Meanwhile, Carol realizes just what her fiancée envisions as married life.

programme.title: Archie And The Editorial
programme.desc: Archie gets fed up with a television editorial when this week's issue concerns gun control. Archie rails against gun control, battles it out with Mike and ultimately decides to go on the TV show with a rebuttal. Later, Archie meets his two biggest supporters -a pair of stickup artist who rob him at gunpoint.

programme.title: Archie's Fraud
programme.desc: Archie's Sunday job, driving taxi cabs for Bert Munson, has been a fine extra job for additional income. However, Archie has failed to report the extra income on his tax returns. Archie goes down to the IRS to explain, and soon discovers that he will be audited by the IRS. Trying to get out of it, Archie considers bribing the agent.

programme.title: Gloria And The Riddle
programme.desc: Gloria has become friends with a girl named Tammy and they have been organizing a Women's Fair. Male chauvinism is put to the test when Gloria poses a riddle to Mike and Archie who are stumped when they can't come up with the answer. Archie even goes to his drinking buddies for help, but to no avail. However, it is Edith who suddenly comes up with the answer (an answer that Archie doesn't want to hear).

programme.title: The Threat
programme.desc: Archie gets word that an old Army buddy who once saved his life is coming to town with his wife, Bobbi Jo. Archie offers to have them stay at his place. When Bobbi Jo arrives early, Archie gets an eyeful of the voluptuous woman and his guilt over his lust begins to show through to Mike and Gloria. Edith overhears a conversation between Archie, Mike and Gloria and out of concern for her marriage, makes a pivotal decision.

programme.title: Lionel Steps Out
programme.desc: Archie's niece has been visiting the Bunkers and having a great time. She's even made friends with Lionel and loves going out on the town with him. However, a picture taken of the two of them becomes the thing that clues Archie in on their blossoming relationship and he hits the roof, citing that people should stick to their own race.

programme.title: The Bunkers And The Swingers
programme.desc: Edith finds a magazine on the subway and reads an ad in the "swap" section. Not realizing what is actually being asked for, Edith assumes the couple is looking for friendship. Mike and Gloria, in a rush, try to explain what they really want. However, it isn't until the couple is actually standing in the Bunker's living room that Edith realizes, with a little help from Louise, that they are "swingers" and they want to swap with Archie and Edith.

programme.title: Mike's Appendix
programme.desc: Both Archie and Gloria come home in bad moods after a hard day's work. This leads to yet another argument over women's liberation. Meanwhile, Mike has been having a pain in the side and the doctor tells him that he needs to have an appendectomy. This leads Archie and Gloria's argument to the proficiency of women doctors. Mike would rather have a more expensive male doctor rather than a cheaper woman doctor.

programme.title: Edith Flips Her Wig
programme.desc: Edith comes home from shopping in a terrible state; it seems she had been arrested for shoplifting. Apparently, she had walked out of a store, wearing a wig, while trying to give bus directions to a woman. Edith becomes convinced that she has become a kleptomaniac, after finding that she also "stole" a necklace. Edith begins to obsess and Mike suggests that she see a psychiatrist, however, Edith has something in mind herself; she visits a priest.

programme.title: The Locket
programme.desc: Edith's antique locket is missing and Archie sees it as a way to collect from the insurance company to buy a new television set. Confident of getting the insurance money, Archie orders a new color set, only to have an insurance adjuster arrive at the house to question the claim.

programme.title: Mike Comes Into Money
programme.desc: Mike comes into some money when his Uncle Alex dies and leaves him $275. Archie tries to persuade Mike into giving the money to him for room and board, but Mike would rather give it to George McGovern's presidential campaign. This sparks a big feud, leading to a dinner separation: Gloria and Mike buy and cook their own food while Edith and Archie do the same.

programme.title: Flashback: Mike And Gloria's Wedding
programme.desc: Edith is in a celebratory mood for Mike and Gloria's second wedding anniversary. While sitting around the dinner table enjoying a special cake, the family recalls Mike and Gloria's wedding. They also recall how Mike's Uncle Casimir met Archie and how they butted heads over who would perform the ceremony.

programme.title: Flashback: Mike And Gloria's Wedding - Pt. Ii
programme.desc: The family continues to reminisce on Mike and Gloria's second wedding anniversary and they recall the actual day Mike and Gloria got married. As Archie and Mike's Uncle Casimir continue to butt heads, Edith must gather up strength to tell Gloria about 'the birds and the bees'.

programme.title: Hazel And The Lovebirds
programme.desc: Deirdre allows her daughter Nancy to move back from private school on the condition that she date someone other than Hazel's nephew, Eddy. Susan Silo appears as Gabriella, the Johnson's new maid. Robert Hogan appears as Bud, a gas station attendant that Deirdre also won't let Nancy date.

programme.title: Top Secret
programme.desc: Hazel takes Stan's daughter to meet a visiting senator (Larry Gates), but the girl inadvertently walks out with highly classified documents.

programme.title: The Sunshine Girls Quartette
programme.desc: Hazel, Rosie (Maudie Prickett) and a pair of Sunshine Girls team with Mitch (Dub Taylor) to form a musical group. However, a surprise birthday party for Mr. Griffin prevents Hazel from attending an audition in front of a traveling talent scout (Jean Willes).

programme.title: A Good Example For Harold
programme.desc: George uses a white lie to get out of meeting with an insurance agent. When Hazel catches Harold using the same type of white lie, she says everyone has to stop using lies. Hazel then has to tell Mr. Boyle (Philip Ober), the creator of cleaning product and a potential client for George, that the product doesn't work.

programme.title: Hazel's Highland Fling
programme.desc: Hazel attempts to stop Mr. Griffin from continually inviting himself for dinner because his new Scottish cook Angie is bad. She came to America to find her longtime boyfriend Gordie (James Doohan) and Hazel helps reunite the two of them.

programme.title: Ain't Walter Nice?
programme.desc: When Hazel's nephew Walter (Frank Aletter), a self-described promoter, arrives with hopes of obtaining a large investment from Mr. Griffin, George and Dorothy begin to question his motives.

programme.title: Mr. Griffin Throws A Wedding
programme.desc: Mr. Griffin takes it upon himself to plan the wedding of his nephew Pete (Dick Sargent) and George's stenographer, Maggie. When he also buys them a house and tries to find a new profession for Pete, Hazel steps in.

programme.title: Hazel And The Stockholders' Meeting
programme.desc: After Hazel's new Davidson vacuum cleaner stops working almost immediately, she takes it upon herself to attend the company's stockholder meeting to see that justice is done. Max Showalter appears as a member of the Davidson company. Byron Foulger appears as Mr. Zimmerman, the vacuum repair man.

programme.title: Hazel's Day Off
programme.desc: Busy Mr. Arden (James Westerfield) will only donate land for a new playground if the contract is drawn up on a Sunday afternoon. When he arrives, Hazel speaks her mind. William Schallert makes an appearance as Arden's attorney.

programme.title: I Been Singing All My Life
programme.desc: George's sister Deirdre is planning a talent show to raise money for the local children's hospital. Hazel wants to be part of the show, but Deirdre refuses to audition her and her friends. Circumstances force Deirdre to change her mind.

programme.title: The Fire's Never Dead While The Ashes Are Red
programme.desc: After George's former law professor (Vaughn Taylor) writes a bestseller, Hazel finds out that a local bookstore owner (Lurene Tuttle) used to date him long ago and sets out to reunite them.

programme.title: Hazel's Navy Blue Tugboats
programme.desc: Hazel borrows George's car to attend a shoe sale, and along the way is delayed by changing a friend's bicycle tire, giving an expectant father a ride to the hospital, and comforting a lost girl who speaks no English.

programme.title: The Hazel Walk
programme.desc: As Hazel battles both George and Mr. Griffin in trying to save the Pocono Trail from development they have planned, she takes them there on an overnight hiking trip, which helps change some minds.

programme.title: Hazel Digs A Hole For Herself
programme.desc: With Hazel's help, George's bored and depressed mother (Louise Lorimer) begins to develop a career as a professional gardener, an occupation that will undoubtedly cause conflict with her snobby daughter Deirdre.

programme.title: Hazel Sounds Her A
programme.desc: After Sir Horace (Torin Thatcher), the town's new symphony conductor, bans women from the orchestra and fires the daughter of Hazel's friend, Hazel finds out some compelling information on the conductor. This will lead to the friend's daughter being reinstated. Doris Singleton appears as Lady Hobart, Sir Horace's wife.

programme.title: Even as I.O.U.
programme.desc: Horse racing is trouble for the boys.

programme.title: The Spring Festival
programme.desc: Love is in the air as the first day of spring arrives in Hooterville. And who better to lead the town in celebrating it than Lisa. She is resolved to show Oliver just what spring is all about and sets out to produce a Spring festival celebration.

programme.title: Our Son, The Barber
programme.desc: It's a hair-raising episode as Eb (Tom Lester) enrolls in a correspondence course to become a barber. Lisa (Eva Gabor) and Oliver (Eddie Albert) give him the money to learn the trade but learning how to cut hair by mail has its glitches.

programme.title: Oliver's Jaded Past
programme.desc: For a lawyer, Oliver should be more careful about what he signs. Lisa reminds him that two years ago Oliver made her a promise to take her back to New York for a two-week vacation. And she has it in writing� on the bottom of the kitchen drawer.

programme.title: The Hungarian Curse
programme.desc: It's hard to know who has a curse over Oliver, but he sure has his hands full with Lisa and her fellow Hungarian Lazlo Broslav. Lazlo appears with a letter claiming he once saved the life of Lisa's uncle, and now her family must repay the debt.

programme.title: The Rutabaga Story
programme.desc: There's a problem in Hooterville: no farmer seems able to grow a cash crop on account of the quality of the soil. But as it turns out from Oliver's (Eddie Albert) soil and mineral research, the oft misunderstood rutabaga is an ideal choice.

programme.title: A Star Named Arnold Is Born (Part 1)
programme.desc: Green Acres goes Hollywood in this first installment of a two-part season finale. When Arnold the pig appears in a play at the Hooterville Theater, Lisa (Eva Gabor) arranges for an old friend to give Arnold a chance on the big screen.

programme.title: A Star Named Arnold Is Born (Part 2)
programme.desc: It's a swine-sational third season finale for Green Acres, as Oliver and Lisa chaperone Arnold, the Ziffel's talented pig, to Hollywood for his big screen test. There's only one problem, Lisa failed to mention that Arnold is not your normal actor.

programme.title: Guess Who's Not Going To The Luau
programme.desc: Hooterville is abuzz as the townsfolk enter a contest sponsored by Peterson's Poi Juice, with a trip to Hawaii as the top prize. But no one expects the winner to be Arnold, the Ziffels' talented pig. Maybe a pig might fly after all, to the islands!

programme.title: The Rummage Sale
programme.desc: A charity rummage sale asks for donated clothing. Being among the wealthier residents of Hooterville, the organizers look to the Douglases. It isn't long before Oliver has to stop Lisa from giving away his entire wardrobe!

programme.title: Eb's Romance
programme.desc: Love is in the air when Eb is quickly engaged to Loreli Appleby, whom he has only known for a day. Eb tries to impress Loreli's father, a farmer, but in typical fashion, he accidentally ruins his future father-in-law's cabbage crop.

programme.title: Hail To The Fire Chief
programme.desc: When Fire Chief Joe Carson is looking for a way to go to Miami, he hires Oliver as his assistant, hoping Oliver will pay for the trip. The plan backfires, no pun intended, when Oliver starts pointing out what a poor job the chief has been doing.

programme.title: The Candidate
programme.desc: The highways in Hooterville are in dire need of repair. Oliver is fed up and believes he can make a difference by complaining to his state representative, Ben Hanks. But Hanks' popularity proves greater than his sense of responsibility.

programme.title: Handy Lessons
programme.desc: Lisa feels like she should be contributing more and wants to start working in Oliver's construction business. Lisa is like a fish out of water as she arrives to the Monroes' workshop in overalls and causes one problem after another.

programme.title: A Husband For Eleanor
programme.desc: Lisa plays bovine matchmaker when Oliver and Lisa decide it is time for their female cow, Eleanor, to start providing milk for the farm. Oliver makes arrangements for Eleanor to mooooove in with the neighbor's bull, but Lisa has other ideas.

programme.title: Old Mail Day
programme.desc: The townsfolk look forward to "Old Mail Day", when once a year undelivered mail is handed out. The delayed mail includes a letter all the way from 1917! Oliver is upset by the poor service, but his complaints land with more impact than he intended.

programme.title: The Agricultural Student
programme.desc: When word spreads that an agricultural student is coming to Hooterville to study with county agent Hank Kimball, no one seems to be interested. However, things change when the folks discover that the student is an attractive blonde.

programme.title: How Hooterville Was Floundered
programme.desc: This episode tells the unusual tale of Hooterville's founding. As the town prepares for its centennial, Oliver announces a reenactment of the town's origins and casts himself and Lisa in the roles of Horace and Doris Hooter.

programme.title: Fester Goes On A Diet
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez see Fester conferring with TV muscleman Jack LaGrann (Jack LaLanne), and think that Fester is planning a television career. However, Uncle Fester confides to Lurch that he has to go on a secret diet and exercise.

programme.title: The Great Treasure Hunt
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez find Grandfather Pegleg's treasure map and summon Captain Grimby. They hope to charter his boat for the treasure hunt. But after meeting the Captain, Morticia is careful to keep the nature of the mission secret.

programme.title: Ophelia Finds Romance
programme.desc: Ophelia Frump is madly in love with Horatio Bartholomew, the man who has everything, except the truth about her relatives. Morticia, not convinced by Bartholomew, persuades playboy Cousin Itt to stir the embers of his past romance with Ophelia.

programme.title: Pugsley's Allowance
programme.desc: Pugsley shocks his parents when he gives up his new atomic reactor as kid stuff and announces that he wants to find his own job. His first try is as a medical assistant to Dr. Bird. He turns up, scalpel in hand, ready to help during an operation.

programme.title: Happy Birthday, Grandma Frump
programme.desc: Grandma Frump (guest star Margaret Hamilton) comes to the mistaken conclusion that her daughter, Morticia (Carolyn Jones), and Gomez (John Astin) plan to pack her off to an old folks home. Actually, they plan to send her to a beauty farm.

programme.title: Morticia, The Decorator
programme.desc: Morticia, in order to be part of the National Beautification Program, decides to become a home decorator and offers her services to the neighbors. Next door neighbor, and insurance agent, Joe Digby hires Morticia in hopes of selling them insurance.

programme.title: Ophelia Visits Morticia
programme.desc: Ophelia taunts Uncle Fester into announcing that he is going to join the Peace Corps. Fester feels he's a cinch to be accepted because Gomez plans to pull strings in Washington on his behalf. The entire family agrees to help him study for the exam.

programme.title: Addams Cum Laude
programme.desc: Allyn Joslyn guest-stars as Sam Hilliard, a former truant officer who now heads a private school where Wednesday and Pugsley are enrolled. When Hilliard breaks under the strain of dealing with the Addams children, Gomez buys the school.

programme.title: Cat Addams
programme.desc: Dr. Marvin P. Gunderson is summoned to examine Kitty Kat, the ailing lion pet of the Addams Family. Morticia decides that it is the doctor who needs help. To build his confidence, human members of the family pretend to be ill so he can affect cures.

programme.title: Lurch's Little Helper
programme.desc: Morticia's concern that Lurch is overworked leads Gomez to the operating table to assemble a custom-built secondary butler. Lurch lets the robot do all the work and then begins to feel that he isn't needed anymore.

programme.title: The Addams Policy
programme.desc: When Uncle Fester incinerates the family's stuffed bear with his flamethrower, Morticia reports the accident to the insurance company. Mr. Henson, the head of the company, sends his assistant, Mr. Digby, to handle the case.

programme.title: Lurch's Grand Romance
programme.desc: Lurch suffers from unrequited love over Tiny Trivia (guest star Diane Jergens), Morticia's visiting school chum who only has eyes for show business. The entire family decides to try to turn their stolid butler in to a new personality.

programme.title: Ophelia's Career
programme.desc: When Ophelia laments that she has been jilted, Morticia suggests a career as an alternative to marriage. She tries chemistry first (with explosive results) then turns to operatic singing. Cousin Itt is summoned to coach her to stardom.

programme.title: The Addams Family Goes To School
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez decide to keep Wednesday and Pugsley home from school after Morticia sees the fairy tales they have to read. When the truant officer comes to investigate, he is shocked at what he finds.

programme.title: Morticia And The Psychiatrist
programme.desc: Morticia calls a psychiatrist after she fears Pugsley is becoming normal after he abandons his pet octopus for a puppy and is seen wearing a Boy Scout uniform, playing with a baseball bat, and helping an old lady cross the street.

programme.title: Fester's Punctured Romance
programme.desc: Uncle Fester, who has become lonely, convinces Gomez and Morticia that it is time for him to find a bride. He decides to put an ad in the newspaper, looking for love. But things go awry when a door-to-door saleswoman comes by.

programme.title: Gomez, The Politician
programme.desc: Election fever grips the Addams household, as Gomez (John Astin) supports the council candidate whose campaign promise is to drain the town's disgusting bogs. Morticia (Carolyn Jones), who likes "the lovely bogs," labels the candidate an extremist.

programme.title: Movie The Perfect Mate
programme.desc: Hope (Brittany Lucio) is a control freak and a perfectionist and nothing would please her more than bringing the perfect Christian future husband home for the weekend to meet her father Reverend Adams (Lawrence Hilton-Jacobs), her mother Mrs. Adams (Jackee Harry), and sister Cherish (KD Aubert). On the day they're scheduled to leave she's dumped by her fiancé. That's when she enlists the help of her platonic male friend, Jesse (Chico Benymon), who is the epitome of everything ungodly, to impersonate her ex, the upcoming pastor. While there, he learns to appreciate family and faith in this hilarious Christian Romance Comedy.
